1.text: Now it came to pass in the days<S>3117</S> when the judges<S>8199</S> ruled<S>8199</S>, that there was a famine<S>7458</S> in the land<S>776</S>. And a certain man<S>376</S> of Bethlehemjudah<S>1035</S> <S>3063</S> went<S>3212</S> to sojourn<S>1481</S> in the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S>, he, and his wife<S>802</S>, and his two<S>8147</S> sons<S>1121</S>. <sup>ruled: Heb. judged</sup>

And the name<S>8034</S> of the man<S>376</S> was Elimelech<S>458</S>, and the name<S>8034</S> of his wife<S>802</S> Naomi<S>5281</S>, and the name<S>8034</S> of his two<S>8147</S> sons<S>1121</S> Mahlon<S>4248</S> and Chilion<S>3630</S>, Ephrathites<S>673</S> of Bethlehemjudah<S>1035</S> <S>3063</S>. And they came<S>935</S> into the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S>, and continued there. <sup>continued: Heb. were</sup>
And Elimelech<S>458</S> Naomi's<S>5281</S> husband<S>376</S> died<S>4191</S>; and she was left<S>7604</S>, and her two<S>8147</S> sons<S>1121</S>.

And they took<S>5375</S> them wives<S>802</S> of the women<S>802</S> of Moab<S>4125</S>; the name<S>8034</S> of the one<S>259</S> was Orpah<S>6204</S>, and the name<S>8034</S> of the other<S>8145</S> Ruth<S>7327</S>: and they dwelled<S>3427</S> there about ten<S>6235</S> years<S>8141</S>.
And Mahlon<S>4248</S> and Chilion<S>3630</S> died<S>4191</S> also both<S>8147</S> of them; and the woman<S>802</S> was left<S>7604</S> of her two<S>8147</S> sons<S>3206</S> and her husband<S>376</S>.

Then she arose<S>6965</S> with her daughters in law<S>3618</S>, that she might return<S>7725</S> from the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S>: for she had heard<S>8085</S> in the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S> how that the LORD<S>3068</S> had visited<S>6485</S> his people<S>5971</S> in giving<S>5414</S> them bread<S>3899</S>.

Wherefore she went forth<S>3318</S> out of the place<S>4725</S> where she was, and her two<S>8147</S> daughters in law<S>3618</S> with her; and they went<S>3212</S> on the way<S>1870</S> to return<S>7725</S> unto the land<S>776</S> of Judah<S>3063</S>.

And Naomi<S>5281</S> said<S>559</S> unto her two<S>8147</S> daughters in law<S>3618</S>, Go<S>3212</S>, return<S>7725</S> each<S>802</S> to her mother's<S>517</S> house<S>1004</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> deal<S>6213</S> kindly<S>2617</S> with you, as ye have dealt<S>6213</S> with the dead<S>4191</S>, and with me.

The LORD<S>3068</S> grant<S>5414</S> you that ye may find<S>4672</S> rest<S>4496</S>, each<S>802</S> of you in the house<S>1004</S> of her husband<S>376</S>. Then she kissed<S>5401</S> them; and they lifted up<S>5375</S> their voice<S>6963</S>, and wept<S>1058</S>.
And they said<S>559</S> unto her, Surely we will return<S>7725</S> with thee unto thy people<S>5971</S>.

And Naomi<S>5281</S> said<S>559</S>, Turn again<S>7725</S>, my daughters<S>1323</S>: why will ye go<S>3212</S> with me? are there yet any more sons<S>1121</S> in my womb<S>4578</S>, that they may be your husbands<S>582</S>?

Turn again<S>7725</S>, my daughters<S>1323</S>, go<S>3212</S> your way; for I am too old<S>2204</S> to have an husband<S>376</S>. If I should say<S>559</S>, I have<S>3426</S> hope<S>8615</S>, if I should have an husband<S>376</S> also to night<S>3915</S>, and should also bear<S>3205</S> sons<S>1121</S>; <sup>should have: or were with an husband</sup>

Would ye tarry<S>7663</S> for them<S>3860</S> till they were grown<S>1431</S>? would ye stay<S>5702</S> for them<S>3860</S> from having husbands<S>376</S>? nay, my daughters<S>1323</S>; for it grieveth<S>4843</S> me much<S>3966</S> for your sakes that the hand<S>3027</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is gone out<S>3318</S> against me. <sup>tarry: Heb. hope</sup> <sup>it grieveth: Heb. I have much bitterness</sup>
And they lifted up<S>5375</S> their voice<S>6963</S>, and wept again<S>1058</S>: and Orpah<S>6204</S> kissed<S>5401</S> her mother in law<S>2545</S>; but Ruth<S>7327</S> clave<S>1692</S> unto her.

And she said<S>559</S>, Behold, thy sister in law<S>2994</S> is gone back<S>7725</S> unto her people<S>5971</S>, and unto her gods<S>430</S>: return<S>7725</S> thou after<S>310</S> thy sister in law<S>2994</S>.

And Ruth<S>7327</S> said<S>559</S>, Intreat<S>6293</S> me not to leave<S>5800</S> thee, or to return<S>7725</S> from following after<S>310</S> thee: for whither thou goest<S>3212</S>, I will go<S>3212</S>; and where thou lodgest<S>3885</S>, I will lodge<S>3885</S>: thy people<S>5971</S> shall be my people<S>5971</S>, and thy God<S>430</S> my God<S>430</S>: <sup>Intreat: or, Be not against me</sup>

Where thou diest<S>4191</S>, will I die<S>4191</S>, and there will I be buried<S>6912</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> do<S>6213</S> so to me, and more<S>3254</S> also, if ought but death<S>4194</S> part<S>6504</S> thee and me.
When she saw<S>7200</S> that she was stedfastly minded<S>553</S> to go<S>3212</S> with her, then she left<S>2308</S> speaking<S>1696</S> unto her. <sup>was: Heb. strengthened herself</sup>

So they two<S>8147</S> went<S>3212</S> until they came<S>935</S> to Bethlehem<S>1035</S>. And it came to pass, when they were come<S>935</S> to Bethlehem<S>1035</S>, that all the city<S>5892</S> was moved<S>1949</S> about them, and they said<S>559</S>, Is this Naomi<S>5281</S>?

And she said<S>559</S> unto them, Call<S>7121</S> me not Naomi<S>5281</S>, call<S>7121</S> me Mara<S>4755</S>: for the Almighty<S>7706</S> hath dealt very<S>3966</S> bitterly<S>4843</S> with me. <sup>Naomi: that is, Pleasant</sup> <sup>Mara: that is, Bitter</sup>

I went out<S>1980</S> full<S>4392</S>, and the LORD<S>3068</S> hath brought me home again<S>7725</S> empty<S>7387</S>: why then call<S>7121</S> ye me Naomi<S>5281</S>, seeing the LORD<S>3068</S> hath testified<S>6030</S> against me, and the Almighty<S>7706</S> hath afflicted<S>7489</S> me?

So Naomi<S>5281</S> returned<S>7725</S>, and Ruth<S>7327</S> the Moabitess<S>4125</S>, her daughter in law<S>3618</S>, with her, which returned<S>7725</S> out of the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S>: and they came<S>935</S> to Bethlehem<S>1035</S> in the beginning<S>8462</S> of barley<S>8184</S> harvest<S>7105</S>.

2.text: And Naomi<S>5281</S> had a kinsman<S>4129</S> <S>3045</S> of her husband's<S>376</S>, a mighty<S>1368</S> man<S>376</S> of wealth<S>2428</S>, of the family<S>4940</S> of Elimelech<S>458</S>; and his name<S>8034</S> was Boaz<S>1162</S>. <sup>Boaz: Gr. Booz</sup>

And Ruth<S>7327</S> the Moabitess<S>4125</S> said<S>559</S> unto Naomi<S>5281</S>, Let me now go<S>3212</S> to the field<S>7704</S>, and glean<S>3950</S> ears of corn<S>7641</S> after<S>310</S> him in whose sight<S>5869</S> I shall find<S>4672</S> grace<S>2580</S>. And she said<S>559</S> unto her, Go<S>3212</S>, my daughter<S>1323</S>.

And she went<S>3212</S>, and came<S>935</S>, and gleaned<S>3950</S> in the field<S>7704</S> after<S>310</S> the reapers<S>7114</S>: and her hap<S>4745</S> was to light<S>7136</S> on a part<S>2513</S> of the field<S>7704</S> belonging unto Boaz<S>1162</S>, who was of the kindred<S>4940</S> of Elimelech<S>458</S>. <sup>hap: Heb. hap happened</sup>

And, behold, Boaz<S>1162</S> came<S>935</S> from Bethlehem<S>1035</S>, and said<S>559</S> unto the reapers<S>7114</S>, The LORD<S>3068</S> be with you. And they answered<S>559</S> him, The LORD<S>3068</S> bless<S>1288</S> thee.
Then said<S>559</S> Boaz<S>1162</S> unto his servant<S>5288</S> that was set<S>5324</S> over the reapers<S>7114</S>, Whose damsel<S>5291</S> is this?

And the servant<S>5288</S> that was set<S>5324</S> over the reapers<S>7114</S> answered<S>6030</S> and said<S>559</S>, It is the Moabitish<S>4125</S> damsel<S>5291</S> that came back<S>7725</S> with Naomi<S>5281</S> out of the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S>:

And she said<S>559</S>, I pray you, let me glean<S>3950</S> and gather<S>622</S> after<S>310</S> the reapers<S>7114</S> among the sheaves<S>6016</S>: so she came<S>935</S>, and hath continued<S>5975</S> even<S>227</S> from the morning<S>1242</S> until now, that she tarried<S>3427</S> a little<S>4592</S> in the house<S>1004</S>.

Then said<S>559</S> Boaz<S>1162</S> unto Ruth<S>7327</S>, Hearest<S>8085</S> thou not, my daughter<S>1323</S>? Go<S>3212</S> not to glean<S>3950</S> in another<S>312</S> field<S>7704</S>, neither go<S>5674</S> from hence, but abide<S>1692</S> here fast by my maidens<S>5291</S>:

Let thine eyes<S>5869</S> be on the field<S>7704</S> that they do reap<S>7114</S>, and go<S>1980</S> thou after<S>310</S> them: have I not charged<S>6680</S> the young men<S>5288</S> that they shall not touch<S>5060</S> thee? and when thou art athirst<S>6770</S>, go<S>1980</S> unto the vessels<S>3627</S>, and drink<S>8354</S> of that which the young men<S>5288</S> have drawn<S>7579</S>.

Then she fell<S>5307</S> on her face<S>6440</S>, and bowed<S>7812</S> herself to the ground<S>776</S>, and said<S>559</S> unto him, Why have I found<S>4672</S> grace<S>2580</S> in thine eyes<S>5869</S>, that thou shouldest take knowledge<S>5234</S> of me, seeing I am a stranger<S>5237</S>?

And Boaz<S>1162</S> answered<S>6030</S> and said<S>559</S> unto her, It hath fully<S>5046</S> been shewed<S>5046</S> me, all that thou hast done<S>6213</S> unto thy mother in law<S>2545</S> since<S>310</S> the death<S>4194</S> of thine husband<S>376</S>: and how thou hast left<S>5800</S> thy father<S>1</S> and thy mother<S>517</S>, and the land<S>776</S> of thy nativity<S>4138</S>, and art come<S>3212</S> unto a people<S>5971</S> which thou knewest<S>3045</S> not heretofore<S>8543</S> <S>8032</S>.

The LORD<S>3068</S> recompense<S>7999</S> thy work<S>6467</S>, and a full<S>8003</S> reward<S>4909</S> be given thee of the LORD<S>3068</S> God<S>430</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, under whose wings<S>3671</S> thou art come<S>935</S> to trust<S>2620</S>.

Then she said<S>559</S>, Let me find<S>4672</S> favour<S>2580</S> in thy sight<S>5869</S>, my lord<S>113</S>; for that thou hast comforted<S>5162</S> me, and for that thou hast spoken<S>1696</S> friendly<S>3820</S> unto thine handmaid<S>8198</S>, though I be not like unto one<S>259</S> of thine handmaidens<S>8198</S>. <sup>Let me: or, I find</sup> <sup>friendly: Heb. to the heart</sup>

And Boaz<S>1162</S> said<S>559</S> unto her, At mealtime<S>6256</S> <S>400</S> come<S>5066</S> thou hither<S>1988</S>, and eat<S>398</S> of the bread<S>3899</S>, and dip<S>2881</S> thy morsel<S>6595</S> in the vinegar<S>2558</S>. And she sat<S>3427</S> beside<S>6654</S> the reapers<S>7114</S>: and he reached<S>6642</S> her parched<S>7039</S> corn, and she did eat<S>398</S>, and was sufficed<S>7646</S>, and left<S>3498</S>.

And when she was risen up<S>6965</S> to glean<S>3950</S>, Boaz<S>1162</S> commanded<S>6680</S> his young men<S>5288</S>, saying<S>559</S>, Let her glean<S>3950</S> even among the sheaves<S>6016</S>, and reproach<S>3637</S> her not: <sup>reproach: Heb. shame her not</sup>
And let fall<S>7997</S> also some of the handfuls<S>6653</S> of purpose<S>7997</S> for her, and leave<S>5800</S> them, that she may glean<S>3950</S> them, and rebuke<S>1605</S> her not.
So she gleaned<S>3950</S> in the field<S>7704</S> until even<S>6153</S>, and beat out<S>2251</S> that she had gleaned<S>3950</S>: and it was about an ephah<S>374</S> of barley<S>8184</S>.

And she took it up<S>5375</S>, and went<S>935</S> into the city<S>5892</S>: and her mother in law<S>2545</S> saw<S>7200</S> what she had gleaned<S>3950</S>: and she brought forth<S>3318</S>, and gave<S>5414</S> to her that she had reserved<S>3498</S> after she was sufficed<S>7648</S>.

And her mother in law<S>2545</S> said<S>559</S> unto her, Where hast thou gleaned<S>3950</S> to day<S>3117</S>? and where<S>375</S> wroughtest<S>6213</S> thou? blessed<S>1288</S> be he that did take knowledge<S>5234</S> of thee. And she shewed<S>5046</S> her mother in law<S>2545</S> with whom she had wrought<S>6213</S>, and said<S>559</S>, The man's<S>376</S> name<S>8034</S> with whom I wrought<S>6213</S> to day<S>3117</S> is Boaz<S>1162</S>.

And Naomi<S>5281</S> said<S>559</S> unto her daughter in law<S>3618</S>, Blessed<S>1288</S> be he of the LORD<S>3068</S>, who hath not left off<S>5800</S> his kindness<S>2617</S> to the living<S>2416</S> and to the dead<S>4191</S>. And Naomi<S>5281</S> said<S>559</S> unto her, The man<S>376</S> is near of kin<S>7138</S> unto us, one of our next kinsmen<S>1350</S>. <sup>one of: or, one that hath right to redeem</sup>

And Ruth<S>7327</S> the Moabitess<S>4125</S> said<S>559</S>, He said<S>559</S> unto me also, Thou shalt keep fast<S>1692</S> by my young men<S>5288</S>, until they have ended<S>3615</S> all my harvest<S>7105</S>.

And Naomi<S>5281</S> said<S>559</S> unto Ruth<S>7327</S> her daughter in law<S>3618</S>, It is good<S>2896</S>, my daughter<S>1323</S>, that thou go out<S>3318</S> with his maidens<S>5291</S>, that they meet<S>6293</S> thee not in any other<S>312</S> field<S>7704</S>. <sup>meet: or, fall upon thee</sup>

So she kept fast<S>1692</S> by the maidens<S>5291</S> of Boaz<S>1162</S> to glean<S>3950</S> unto the end<S>3615</S> of barley<S>8184</S> harvest<S>7105</S> and of wheat<S>2406</S> harvest<S>7105</S>; and dwelt<S>3427</S> with her mother in law<S>2545</S>.

3.text: Then Naomi<S>5281</S> her mother in law<S>2545</S> said<S>559</S> unto her, My daughter<S>1323</S>, shall I not seek<S>1245</S> rest<S>4494</S> for thee, that it may be well<S>3190</S> with thee?

And now is not Boaz<S>1162</S> of our kindred<S>4130</S>, with whose maidens<S>5291</S> thou wast? Behold, he winnoweth<S>2219</S> barley<S>8184</S> to night<S>3915</S> in the threshingfloor<S>1637</S>.

Wash<S>7364</S> thyself therefore, and anoint<S>5480</S> thee, and put<S>7760</S> thy raiment<S>8071</S> upon thee, and get thee down<S>3381</S> to the floor<S>1637</S>: but make not thyself known<S>3045</S> unto the man<S>376</S>, until he shall have done<S>3615</S> eating<S>398</S> and drinking<S>8354</S>.

And it shall be, when he lieth down<S>7901</S>, that thou shalt mark<S>3045</S> the place<S>4725</S> where he shall lie<S>7901</S>, and thou shalt go in<S>935</S>, and uncover<S>1540</S> his feet<S>4772</S>, and lay thee down<S>7901</S>; and he will tell<S>5046</S> thee what thou shalt do<S>6213</S>. <sup>uncover: or, lift up the clothes that are on</sup>
And she said<S>559</S> unto her, All that thou sayest<S>559</S> unto me I will do<S>6213</S>.
And she went down<S>3381</S> unto the floor<S>1637</S>, and did<S>6213</S> according to all that her mother in law<S>2545</S> bade<S>6680</S> her.

And when Boaz<S>1162</S> had eaten<S>398</S> and drunk<S>8354</S>, and his heart<S>3820</S> was merry<S>3190</S>, he went<S>935</S> to lie down<S>7901</S> at the end<S>7097</S> of the heap of corn<S>6194</S>: and she came<S>935</S> softly<S>3909</S>, and uncovered<S>1540</S> his feet<S>4772</S>, and laid her down<S>7901</S>.

And it came to pass at midnight<S>2677</S> <S>3915</S>, that the man<S>376</S> was afraid<S>2729</S>, and turned<S>3943</S> himself: and, behold, a woman<S>802</S> lay<S>7901</S> at his feet<S>4772</S>. <sup>turned: or, took hold on</sup>

And he said<S>559</S>, Who art thou? And she answered<S>559</S>, I am Ruth<S>7327</S> thine handmaid<S>519</S>: spread<S>6566</S> therefore thy skirt<S>3671</S> over thine handmaid<S>519</S>; for thou art a near kinsman<S>1350</S>. <sup>a near: or, one that hath right to redeem</sup>

And he said<S>559</S>, Blessed<S>1288</S> be thou of the LORD<S>3068</S>, my daughter<S>1323</S>: for thou hast shewed<S>3190</S> more kindness<S>2617</S> in the latter end<S>314</S> than at the beginning<S>7223</S>, inasmuch as thou followedst<S>3212</S> <S>310</S> not<S>1115</S> young men<S>970</S>, whether poor<S>1800</S> or rich<S>6223</S>.

And now, my daughter<S>1323</S>, fear<S>3372</S> not; I will do<S>6213</S> to thee all that thou requirest<S>559</S>: for all the city<S>8179</S> of my people<S>5971</S> doth know<S>3045</S> that thou art a virtuous<S>2428</S> woman<S>802</S>. <sup>city: Heb. gate</sup>
And now it is true<S>551</S> that I am thy near kinsman<S>1350</S>: howbeit there is<S>3426</S> a kinsman<S>1350</S> nearer<S>7138</S> than I.

Tarry<S>3885</S> this night<S>3915</S>, and it shall be in the morning<S>1242</S>, that if he will perform unto thee the part of a kinsman<S>1350</S>, well<S>2896</S>; let him do the kinsman's part<S>1350</S>: but if he will<S>2654</S> not do the part of a kinsman<S>1350</S> to thee, then will I do the part of a kinsman<S>1350</S> to thee, as the LORD<S>3068</S> liveth<S>2416</S>: lie down<S>7901</S> until the morning<S>1242</S>.

And she lay<S>7901</S> at his feet<S>4772</S> until the morning<S>1242</S>: and she rose up<S>6965</S> before<S>2958</S> one<S>376</S> could know<S>5234</S> another<S>7453</S>. And he said<S>559</S>, Let it not be known<S>3045</S> that a woman<S>802</S> came<S>935</S> into the floor<S>1637</S>.

Also he said<S>559</S>, Bring<S>3051</S> the vail<S>4304</S> that thou hast upon thee, and hold<S>270</S> it. And when she held<S>270</S> it, he measured<S>4058</S> six<S>8337</S> measures of barley<S>8184</S>, and laid<S>7896</S> it on her: and she went<S>935</S> into the city<S>5892</S>. <sup>vail: or, sheet, or, apron</sup>

And when she came<S>935</S> to her mother in law<S>2545</S>, she said<S>559</S>, Who art thou, my daughter<S>1323</S>? And she told<S>5046</S> her all that the man<S>376</S> had done<S>6213</S> to her.
And she said<S>559</S>, These six<S>8337</S> measures of barley<S>8184</S> gave<S>5414</S> he me; for he said<S>559</S> to me, Go<S>935</S> not empty<S>7387</S> unto thy mother in law<S>2545</S>.

Then said<S>559</S> she, Sit still<S>3427</S>, my daughter<S>1323</S>, until thou know<S>3045</S> how the matter<S>1697</S> will fall<S>5307</S>: for the man<S>376</S> will not be in rest<S>8252</S>, until<S>518</S> he have finished<S>3615</S> the thing<S>1697</S> this day<S>3117</S>.

4.text: Then went<S>5927</S> Boaz<S>1162</S> up<S>5927</S> to the gate<S>8179</S>, and sat him down<S>3427</S> there: and, behold, the kinsman<S>1350</S> of whom Boaz<S>1162</S> spake<S>1696</S> came<S>5674</S> by; unto whom he said<S>559</S>, Ho<S>1945</S>, such<S>6423</S> a one<S>492</S>! turn aside<S>5493</S>, sit down<S>3427</S> here. And he turned aside<S>5493</S>, and sat down<S>3427</S>.
And he took<S>3947</S> ten<S>6235</S> men<S>582</S> of the elders<S>2205</S> of the city<S>5892</S>, and said<S>559</S>, Sit ye down<S>3427</S> here. And they sat down<S>3427</S>.
